alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google with two promotions, scaled global commercial operations and transformation programs; experience building and coaching teams in a consumption-based business context
alignments.job_requirement: Build, lead, and scale the pre-sales (Solutions Architect) team, including hiring, coaching, performance management, and career development

alignments.candidate_evidence: Commercial Operations leader with experience in forecasting, reporting cadence, territory-budget management, and revenue-leakage control; translated to Revenue Operations domain
alignments.job_requirement: Own pre-sales ROI and effectiveness, including coverage models, prioritisation, and impact on win-rate, sales cycles, and customer adoption

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program experience with hands-on execution and cross-functional leadership; product operations background includes UAT, rollout, and process design
alignments.job_requirement: Act as a player-coach in strategic opportunities, balancing hands-on involvement with building repeatable motions and coaching mechanisms

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations at Google Devices and Services; SQL (proficient), BigQuery (working), Looker (dashboards), Tableau; no deep data engineering or consulting brand experience
alignments.job_requirement: Strong background in data engineering, analytics platforms, databases, or data science, gained through engineering, consulting, or technical leadership roles
Fluent German (highly preferred) and fluent English (required)

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google with seniority progression; C-level engagement inferred from scale and scope, not explicitly detailed in profile
alignments.job_requirement: Executive presence with the ability to influence and communicate confidently at C-level

gaps.gap: Deep data engineering, analytics platform, or data science background from engineering or consulting roles
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has analytical toolkit (SQL, BigQuery, Looker) and product operations foundation; however, lack of native data-engineering or consulting brand limits credibility for strategic data/AI positioning with C-level Manufacturing and Energy buyers. Ramp-up risk is real.

gaps.gap: Pre-sales or Solutions Architecture team leadership experience
gaps.mitigation: Commercial Operations and product operations leadership differ structurally from pre-sales team ownership. No stated experience hiring, coaching, or scaling SAs. Team building and player-coach models are transferable but lack SA-specific context.

gaps.gap: Consumption-based SaaS business model depth and expansion forecasting mechanics
gaps.mitigation: Revenue Operations and territory-budget management touch consumption and forecasting; however, no explicit evidence of designing or owning consumption-growth mechanics, upsell models, or PaaS metrics in a usage-driven business.

gaps.gap: Data and AI market positioning and executive POV articulation
gaps.mitigation: Product operations and Google Devices/Services background do not directly map to data/AI market expertise. Building trusted relationships with C-level Manufacturing and Energy buyers requires domain narrative that is not evident.

gaps.gap: Limited consulting or forward-deployed technical engagement background
gaps.mitigation: Honest gaps note limited classic case-team or consulting brand experience. Databricks pre-sales role demands trusted advisor positioning in complex industrial stakeholder environments.

output.level_rationale: Role requires managing a pre-sales team, reporting to Director, Field Engineering, with ownership of coverage, hiring, performance management, and strategic customer relationships at C-level. Manager-of-managers scope combined with P&L ownership (pre-sales ROI, win-rate impact) and regional leadership representation points to L6. Company stage (10,000+ customers, 50% Fortune 500) and Germany strategic-accounts focus reinforce senior seniority anchor.
output.summary: Manager, Field Engineering—Strategic Accounts Germany is an L6 pre-sales leadership role requiring team building, player-coach deal involvement, and C-level relationship ownership in the data/AI market. Candidate brings eight years of Google strategy and operations leadership, two promotions, team-scaling and transformation experience, fluent German, and analytical tools (SQL, BigQuery, Looker). However, critical gaps include no deep data engineering or consulting brand, no pre-sales/Solutions Architecture team experience, limited data/AI market POV, and no stated consumption-growth or usage-driven business model depth. Role family fit is weak; candidate's operations and product-ownership background does not overlap substantially with pre-sales leadership for complex industrial data/AI deals.
uncertainties: Extent of candidate's C-level relationship-building and executive presence at Google (not detailed in profile narrative)
Depth of candidate's data/analytics platform market knowledge and data/AI narrative capability
Whether product operations transformation experience translates meaningfully to pre-sales team scaling and SA coaching
Candidate's appetite and readiness to ramp on data-engineering and Lakehouse/Spark fundamentals to credibly engage strategic Manufacturing and Energy buyers
